title: Buldak Carbonara
tagline: Korean fire noodles, tamed with yolk and parmesan.
description: The viral hack that turns Samyang buldak ramen into a creamy, still-spicy carbonara: egg yolk, parmesan, and a splash of cream emulsified into the fire sauce. All the heat, plus something to hold onto.
ingredients: 2 packs Samyang buldak ramen (original or carbonara flavor)
steps: Boil the noodles 4 minutes (discard nothing yet - you need the water). Reserve 1/2 cup noodle water, then drain.
Whisk egg yolks, parmesan, and cream in a bowl. This is your rescue squad.
Melt butter in the empty pot over medium-low, cook garlic 30 seconds until fragrant, not brown.
Add drained noodles, ONE sauce packet (start there - two is a commitment), and a splash of noodle water. Toss to coat.
Kill the heat. Wait 30 seconds - a raging-hot pot scrambles the yolks.
Pour in the yolk mixture, tossing constantly, adding noodle water a splash at a time until the sauce turns glossy and clings.
Top with green onions, sesame seeds, and the flavor-flake packet if your pack has one.
notes: The heat math: one packet with the dairy lands around 'very spicy but conversational.' Two packets is the full 2x challenge with a cream buffer.
Off the heat is the whole technique - carbonara logic applies whether it's guanciale or fire sauce.
The buldak 'carbonara' flavor pack is fine, but building it from original + real yolk and parmesan tastes dramatically better.
testedNote: One sauce packet plus the dairy is the correct default; the full two-packet version is a challenge video, not dinner.

verdict.note: The rare stunt-food recipe that's actually a good bowl of noodles.

troubleshooting: Scrambled, grainy sauce → pot was still screaming when the yolks went in; off heat, 30 seconds, keep tossing.
Sauce too thick and gluey → needs noodle water; loosen a splash at a time.
Inedibly hot → you used both packets; next time start with one and add.
Sauce won't cling → pre-grated shelf parmesan doesn't melt; grate a wedge.
